data: Marble fragment damaged on all sides, set in plaster in modern times.
Squared Ε, Α with broken bar. A wavy stroke at l. 3 divides the numeral from the following word.
Original discovery not recorded, but probably from Catania.
Observed by Amico in Museo dei Benedettini and later by Korhonen in Magazzino del cortile, Collezione dei Benedettini (formerly sala VI 94).
I.Sicily with the permission of the Assessorato Regionale dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana - Dipartimento dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana
Castello Ursino, Piazza Federico II di Svevia, 95121 Catania (CT)
V. and Statella Amico E., Catana Ilustrata, vol. 3 (Catanae, 1741)
Le iscrizioni del Museo civico di Catania : storia delle collezioni, cultura epigrafica, edizione
Kalle Korhonen, Le iscrizioni del Museo civico di Catania : storia delle collezioni, cultura epigrafica, edizione (Helsinki: Societas Scientiarum Fennica, 2004)
Le iscrizioni datate della Sicilia paleocristiana
Antonio Ferrua, «Le iscrizioni datate della Sicilia paleocristiana», Kokalos 28–29 (1983 1982): 3–29

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Text based on photograph and on Ferrua's edition ;
  • line.1: The only trace at l. 1 seems to belong neither to a N nor to a Ω. ΝΩΝ was seen by Amico in 1741. ;
  • According to Korhonen, also μετὰ τὴν ὑπατείαν is possible.

English translation

... the Nones of ... in the fifteenth consulate of Theodosius and the fourth of Valentinian, the Augusti.

Italian translation

Marta Fogagnolo

... le None di ... durante il quindicesimo consolato di Teodosio e il quarto di Valentiniano, gli Augusti.

commentary

The inscription is probably the fragment of the end of an epitaph with a consular date: the date on which the deceased died is recorded (the indication Νων[ῶν] must have been accompanied by the day and followed by the month) and the consuls of that year. Ferrua’s supplements are based on the traces at l. 2 ΟΣΙΟ, which could only belong to the name Theodosius and the numeral δ probably indicating the number of the consulate of Theodosius’colleague, both Augusti. The only possibility is then 435 AD, the year of the fifteenth consulate of Theodosius II and the fourth of Valentian III (a date also confirmed by palaeography).

Squared Ε, Α with broken bar. A wavy stroke at l. 3 divides the numeral from the following word.

Text based on photograph and on Ferrua's edition The only trace at l. 1 seems to belong neither to a N nor to a Ω. ΝΩΝ was seen by Amico in 1741. According to Korhonen, also μετὰ τὴν ὑπατείαν is possible.

... the Nones of ... in the fifteenth consulate of Theodosius and the fourth of Valentinian, the Augusti.

... le None di ... durante il quindicesimo consolato di Teodosio e il quarto di Valentiniano, gli Augusti.

The inscription is probably the fragment of the end of an epitaph with a consular date: the date on which the deceased died is recorded (the indication Νων[ῶν] must have been accompanied by the day and followed by the month) and the consuls of that year. Ferrua’s supplements are based on the traces at l. 2 ΟΣΙΟ, which could only belong to the name Theodosius and the numeral δ probably indicating the number of the consulate of Theodosius’colleague, both Augusti. The only possibility is then 435 AD, the year of the fifteenth consulate of Theodosius II and the fourth of Valentian III (a date also confirmed by palaeography).
